arguments.name: explore-aot
arguments.prompt:
Explore the project in /tmp/sv-06687ca135a4400c879af9f51455af9b/before/ to understand what needs to change for AOT (Ahead-of-Time) compilation compatibility in .NET.

Key things to look for:
1. The .csproj file for any AOT-related properties that might be missing (IsAotCompatible, PublishAot, JsonSerializerIsReflectionEnabledByDefault, etc.)
2. Any use of reflection-based serialization (System.Text.Json with reflection, JsonSerializer without source generators)
3. Any use of `MakeGenericType`, `Type.GetType`, or other reflection APIs that break AOT
4. Any `[DynamicallyAccessedMembers]` or `[RequiresUnreferencedCode]` attributes that might be needed
5. Any JsonSerializerContext or source-generated serializer contexts
6. Look at the `Shared/` directory for serialization patterns (especially `IUtf8JsonSerializable.cs`, `ManagedServiceIdentityTypeV3Converter.cs`)
7. Look at `Common/Generated/` and `Resources/Generated/` for auto-generated model code and serialization
8. Look for any `JsonConverter` implementations
9. Check for dynamic code patterns

List ALL files with content that is relevant to AOT compatibility. Show the full content of the .csproj file, any serialization-related files, any files using reflection, and any JsonConverter implementations. Be thorough.
